arts.ttl: Aston Villa team news: Who has Unai Emery selected for Young Boys Champions League game?
arts.sum: Unai Emery names Aston Villa's first-ever Champions League XI for the game at Young Boys, the club's first game in the competition.

arts.ttl: Villa unchanged for Young Boys test
arts.sum: Fixtures Young Boys Vs. Aston Villa - Tue, Sep 17th 2024, 18:45 Unai Emery named an unchanged Aston Villa starting line-up for their UEFA Champions League opener against BSC Young Boys. The Villa manager kept faith with the same XI who came from two goals down to triumph 3-2 over Everton in the Premier League at the weekend. Fit-again Leon Bailey returned to the matchday 22 and was named among the substitutes for the clash at Stadion Wankdorf in Bern. He was joined by matchwinner Jhon Durán, as well as Academy graduates Oliwier Zych, Sil Swinkels and Kadan Young.

arts.ttl: Young Boys vs Aston Villa LIVE match updates from the Wankdorf Stadium
arts.sum: Bottom of the Swiss Super League and without a league win after six games, Young Boys have made a slow start to the campaign but still managed to see off Turkish campions Galatasaray home and away to advance into the league phase. Aston Villa's Champions League adventure starts this evening against Swiss champions BSC Young Boys at the Wankdorf Stadium in Bern (5:45pm kick-off).

arts.ttl: Aston Villa team news vs Young Boys confirmed as Unai Emery names first Champions League XI
arts.sum: Villa starting XI vs Young Boys: Martinez, Bogarde, Konsa, Pau, Digne, Onana, Tielemans, McGinn, Ramsey, Rogers, Watkins. Aston Villa manager Unai Emery has named his starting XI to take on BSC Young Boys in the Champions League this evening (5:45pm kick-off).

arts.ttl: Youth League: BSC Young Boys 2-1 Aston Villa
arts.sum: Fixtures Young Boys Vs. Aston Villa - Tue, Sep 17th 2024, 15:00 Aston Villa fell to a narrow 2-1 defeat against BSC Young Boys in their UEFA Youth League opener in Bern on Tuesday afternoon. Rory Wilson handed the Young Lions the lead midway through the first half at Sportpark Wyler following great work from Ben Broggio. But the hosts levelled courtesy of Felix Emanuel Tsimba’s powerful finish in the 37th minute, ensuring the competitive encounter was all square at the break. Yet it was Young Boys who clinched all three points courtesy of Elio Rufener’s second-half strike, with the visitors unable to force a leveller in Switzerland. Villa are next in action at home to Bayern Munich in the prestigious youth competition, which mirrors the club’s Champions League 'league stage’ but is limited to the first six matchdays. It was a keenly-contested opening quarter on the slick artificial surface as both sides battled for an early upper hand, although defences reigned supreme as clear-cut opportunities were limited. That was until Villa opened the scoring in the 21st minute following excellent work from Broggio. The wide man won the ball in a dangerous area and surged towards goal before seeing his strike parried by Young Boys goalkeeper Jan Uebersax into the path of Wilson, who coolly applied the finishing touch. Boss Josep Gombau was forced into a change shortly afterwards as Kyrie Pierre hobbled off, replaced by Ewan Simpson. And it was the hosts who levelled as half-time approached as Tsimba won a battle to latch on to a ball over the top and his fierce strike got the better of Sam Proctor. Yet the Villa goalkeeper ensured it remained level at the break as he used his legs to deny Tsimba after he found himself clean through. Travis Patterson’s 25-yard free-kick was comfortably collected by Uebersax early in the second half, before George Hemmings fired off target from distance. Omar Khedr was introduced just after the hour mark as both sides continued to threaten. But it was the hosts who took the lead in the 68th minute following a well-worked team move which resulted in Rufener converting from Rhodri Smith. Young Boys substitute Janis Luthi fired wide a clear-cut opportunity to establish a two-goal advantage. Patterson saw penalty appeals waved away as Villa pushed for an equaliser, but it was not to be as their European campaign opened with defeat from a hard-fought encounter. Aston Villa: Proctor, Pierre (Simpson 24), Rowe, Katsukunya, Patterson, Burrowes (Khedr 63), Borland, Hemmings, Broggio, Jimoh-Aloba, Wilson. Subs: Hammond, Asemota, Routh, Monteiro Fortes, Carroll, Mulley, Lynskey. Upcoming Fixtures Aston Villa U21 Vs. Blackburn Rovers U21 - Fri, Oct 10th 2024, 18:00 Manchester City U21 Vs. Aston Villa U21 - Fri, Nov 11th 2024, 19:00 Aston Villa U21 Vs. Newcastle United U21 - Mon, Dec 12th 2024, 19:00

arts.ttl: Young Boys vs Aston Villa LIVE: Team news and line-ups as the Champions League returns tonight
arts.sum: Unai Emery takes charge of Aston Villa’s first match in the European top-flight

arts.ttl: Referee appointed for Aston Villa vs Wolves was told he made an 'incredible' mistake in his last game
arts.sum: The Premier League have appointed Tim Robinson as referee for Aston Villa vs Wolverhampton Wanderers this weekend.

arts.ttl: Acorns to adorn Villa shirts against Young Boys
arts.sum: Aston Villa is delighted to announce that Acorns will adorn the front of the players’ shirts in tonight’s Champions League opener against Young Boys. Principal Partners, Betano, have kindly donated the space back to Aston Villa and the club have, in turn, donated the space to Acorns. The club’s long-standing relationship with the hospice goes back to 2008 when they were a permanent fixture on the shirts. Chris Heck, President of Business Operations at Aston Villa, said: “We are delighted that Betano came to us to offer up the opportunity and ask who are the best beneficiaries of the space. “It was an absolute no-brainer to bring Acorns back onto the front of shirt, an organisation close to the hearts of everyone involved with Aston Villa.” Julio Iglesias, Chief Commercial Officer of Kaizen Gaming, added: “With all partnerships, CSR is a major part of the conversation from the beginning in terms of how we give back. “As part of that, to walk the walk, we have a first step to help Aston Villa; donating the space on the shirt for the first Champions League game to an organisation close to Aston Villa’s hearts.” Vicki Rowles, Director of Fundraising at Acorns Children’s Hospice, said: “We are extremely honoured to have our logo featured on Aston Villa’s shirts for their UEFA Champions League debut against Young Boys. “Help can’t wait for the children and families who desperately need us. Thanks to the support of Aston Villa and the global exposure of the Champions League, we can reach even more families in need. “We’re truly grateful to our friends at Aston Villa for their continued dedication to Acorns. We wish the team the very best of luck for tonight’s game.”

arts.ttl: I lifted the European Cup thanks to 'humble' Aston Villa hero Gary Shaw
arts.sum: As Aston Villa prepare for their first foray back into Europe's elite competition since the days of Gary Shaw - the only Birmingham born player to feature in claret and blue against Bayern Munich in the final in 1982 - the club are mourning the tragic loss of one of their European Cup heroes at the age of just 63. Gary and Patrick Whelan were neighbours in Castle Bromwich and, despite the 15-year age gap, they became friends.

arts.ttl: 'Brilliant': Alan Shearer incredibly impressed by £13m player who Aston Villa got rid of this summer
